title: Add per-resource ETags and conditional requests with 304 responses across REST endpoints
business_context: Mobile app polls endpoints aggressively; bandwidth and battery suffer. Need HTTP caching with ETag/If-None-Match so unchanged resources return 304. Existing endpoints don't emit ETags.
constraints: Existing API consumers must continue working
Server can opt out per endpoint

before_state.description: Endpoints emit full body every time.

reasoning: Compute ETag from a stable field like updatedAt + _id. For collections, hash the list of (id, version) pairs. Middleware compares If-None-Match and short-circuits with 304. Cheaper variant for documents: use updatedAt timestamp.

after_state.description: ETag middleware computing strong tags + 304 short-circuit + opt-out flag.

files.purpose: Reusable ETag middleware computing tag and short-circuiting 304
Endpoints expose ETag via wrapper
Client caches by URL with ETag; returns cached body on 304

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate one controller at a time
Step 3: Update client to send If-None-Match and cache
testing_strategy: Round-trip: first call returns 200+ETag, second with If-None-Match returns 304. Tampered ETag: 200 with new body. Collection: change one doc, assert ETag changes.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would hash the entire response body - expensive per request. Hashing only stable identity (id+timestamp) is sufficient and cheap.

title: Replace synchronous JSON parsing of 50MB request bodies with streaming parser
business_context: Bulk import endpoint accepts JSON arrays up to 50MB. Currently express.json buffers entire body, blocking event loop on parse. Need streaming parse + per-row processing without loading all in memory.
reasoning: Streaming parser like stream-json processes arrays piecewise. Pipeline: req → JSONParser → StreamArray → row handler with concurrency. Validate each row with Zod. Collect errors per row index. Bulk insert in batches of 500.

after_state.description: Streaming JSON parse, batched insert, per-row error reporting.

files.purpose: Streaming parse with batched insert + per-row Zod validation + error report
Skip body parsing for streaming endpoint

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add streaming endpoint at /import behind feature flag
Step 2: Validate with 50MB payload in staging
Step 4: Document expectation that errors come back per row
testing_strategy: 50MB import: assert process RSS stays under 200MB. Mixed valid/invalid rows: assert errors list contains row indexes. Backpressure: simulate slow DB, assert request doesn't drop bytes.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would increase express.json limit - body still buffered. Streaming parse is the structural fix.

title: Add live cursor-aware code search with debounced incremental results
business_context: Code search returns first-page results in 800ms. Users want results as they type. Need incremental search with cancellation and result streaming as the index returns hits.
constraints: Cancelled queries must not waste server resources
Cannot adopt a search service mid-project (continue using Mongo text index)
reasoning: Stream results via Server-Sent Events. Each cursor result emits a chunk. Client uses EventSource and accumulates results. Abort via new query = new connection (browser closes old). Server detects res.closed and aborts cursor iteration. Tradeoff vs WebSocket: SSE is simpler and HTTP-only.

after_state.description: SSE-based streaming search with cursor iteration and abort detection.

files.purpose: SSE stream with cursor + abort on client disconnect + yield to event loop
EventSource consumer with debounce and per-query cancellation
Box with progressive results display

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add streaming endpoint alongside batch
testing_strategy: Cancellation test: type fast, assert previous EventSource closed. Server abort: send Connection: close, assert cursor closed. Result order: assert ascending arrival.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would poll repeatedly - bad for backend and worse for users. They'd skip the abort detection, leaving zombie cursors per dropped connection.

title: Add interactive 3D building viewer with floor isolation and BIM metadata overlay
business_context: Facility management wants to inspect building model: select a floor to isolate, click a room to see HVAC metadata. Current PDF blueprints. Need interactive 3D viewer with BIM data integration.
reasoning: GLB with mesh-per-room. Naming convention 'floor-2.room-201' parsed to group rooms by floor. Isolation: hide non-selected floor groups; ghost the rest. Click sets target group, raycaster picks. Metadata via fetch on click. Dispose all on unmount.

after_state.description: Floor-grouped 3D viewer with raycaster picks and metadata panel.

files.purpose: Building viewer with floor isolation via material opacity and click-driven metadata

migration_strategy: Step 3: Ship viewer for one building
testing_strategy: Isolation test: select floor 2, assert other floor materials transparent. Pick test: click known mesh, assert API fetched and panel rendered. Dispose test: switch buildings, assert no leak.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would set mesh.visible=false to hide other floors - loses contextual reference. Transparency keeps the building shape readable.

title: Add SOC2-friendly access logging covering every tenant data access with searchable schema
business_context: SOC2 audit requires logs of every cross-tenant or sensitive-data access. Currently logs are unstructured. Need structured access logs that auditors can search.
constraints: Must capture actor, target, action, IP, requestId, ts
Must be queryable for compliance reports
Cannot regress request latency by more than 5ms
reasoning: Append-only AccessLog collection with bounded fields. Middleware logs after response. Async write (fire-and-forget) for hot path latency. Indexed by orgId+ts and actorId+ts. Retention via S3 archival (see fs-064).

after_state.description: Structured async access log with append-only writes and indexes for compliance queries.

files.purpose: Structured access log with compliance-relevant indexes
Async access log write after response - off hot path
Compliance query endpoint with common filters

migration_strategy: Step 3: Set up archival per fs-064
testing_strategy: Async write test: measure response time impact, assert < 5ms. Cross-org flag: query that touches another org's resource, assert crossOrg=true. Query test: filter by actorId, assert returns only that actor's events.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would await the log write - adds latency. Fire-and-forget plus a queued archival path is the standard.

title: Migrate from REST to typed RPC layer (tRPC-style) without forcing GraphQL adoption
business_context: Frontend duplicates types from API responses; drift causes runtime errors. Need typed endpoint definitions consumed by frontend without runtime cost. Don't want GraphQL infra.
constraints: Server still exposes REST URLs for external consumers

before_state.description: REST + duplicate types on each side.

reasoning: Define endpoints in shared package with input/output types via Zod. Server runtime uses these schemas for validation; frontend imports them for typing. Lightweight RPC client uses path templates. No GraphQL or tRPC framework - just a small wrapper.

after_state.description: Shared endpoint definitions with Zod; server validates, frontend infers types.

files.purpose: Shared endpoint definitions with input/output schemas
Server uses shared schemas for both validation and response shape
Typed RPC client - returns parsed response with inferred type
Component gets typed response inferred from shared schema

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate one endpoint at a time
Step 3: Build admin dashboard listing endpoints
Step 4: Make tsc fail on response shape drift in CI
testing_strategy: Schema test: server response that violates schema fails parse, returns 500 in dev. Client test: tsc errors when consumer uses non-existent endpoint name.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would generate types from OpenAPI - works but adds a codegen step. Sharing Zod schemas removes the build step.

title: Migrate sprawling Redux store to slice-based normalized stores with code splitting
business_context: Single 4000-line redux store. Slow type compilation. Loading any page bundles the whole store. Need slice-based normalized stores, lazy-injected per route.
constraints: Existing components must work during migration
reasoning: Redux Toolkit with createSlice. Lazy slice injection via reducer manager. Routes inject slices on mount. Normalization with createEntityAdapter. Code-split slice modules.

after_state.description: Slice manager with lazy injection, entity adapters, code-split slices.

files.purpose: Reducer manager allowing lazy injection of slice reducers
Slice with EntityAdapter; self-registers on first import
Importing this file triggers slice registration via postsSlice's injectReducer

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add slice manager + first slice
Step 2: Migrate one slice at a time from monolith
Step 3: Move old combineReducers to inject statically
Step 4: Code-split slice files in webpack
testing_strategy: Lazy inject test: route to /posts, assert posts reducer present after mount. Selector test: dispatch upsert, assert selectAll returns. Code-split test: bundle analyzer shows posts slice in its own chunk.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would convert to Redux Toolkit without slicing - still one giant bundle. Or skip the manager, requiring static combineReducers updates per slice.

title: Add OpenAPI generation from Zod schemas with Swagger UI and contract test fixtures
business_context: Customers want OpenAPI spec for their codegen. Endpoints defined via Zod schemas (per fs-081). Need to derive OpenAPI 3.1 spec automatically and serve Swagger UI.
reasoning: Use zod-to-openapi to convert schemas into OpenAPI components. Compile spec from endpoints map. Serve Swagger UI at /docs. Snapshot the spec in CI to detect breaking changes.

after_state.description: OpenAPI generator + Swagger UI + CI snapshot.

files.purpose: Compiles spec from endpoints map
Serves OpenAPI JSON and Swagger UI
CI test fails on spec drift

migration_strategy: Step 4: Document update workflow for intentional changes
testing_strategy: Schema change: add field, run tests, snapshot diff visible. Round-trip: generate spec, import with codegen tool, assert client compiles.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would handwrite YAML - drifts immediately. Generation from Zod ties spec to runtime behavior.

title: Add 3D molecule viewer with WebGL ribbon rendering for protein chains
business_context: Bio research team needs interactive protein structure viewer. PDB files have backbone atoms + bonds. Need ribbon rendering + atom highlighting + measurements.
reasoning: Parse PDB on backend (or client). For atoms: InstancedMesh of spheres. For backbone ribbon: extract C-alpha atoms, build CatmullRomCurve3, sweep ribbon geometry. Highlight via setColorAt. Measurements via clicked atom positions.

after_state.description: PDB parsed to atoms + ribbon, instanced rendering, interactive selection and measurements.

files.purpose: Instanced atoms + ribbon + click-driven selection and pair distance

migration_strategy: Step 2: Ship viewer for one demo molecule
Step 3: Iterate visuals with bio team
testing_strategy: 5k-atom test: render, assert >55fps. Pick test: click atom, assert highlighted. Distance test: pick two atoms, assert reasonable distance computed.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would render one mesh per atom - 5k draw calls. InstancedMesh + ribbon tube is correct.

title: Add billing-aware soft-deletion with restore window and tenant-tier retention
business_context: Free users get instant hard-delete; paid users have a 30-day soft-delete with restore. Currently all deletes are hard. Need tier-aware deletion policy.
constraints: Existing public API contract for DELETE must remain
Restore endpoint visible only to paid users
Periodic purge job cleans expired soft-deletes
reasoning: Add deletedAt timestamp. Free tier: hard delete. Paid: set deletedAt, queries filter out where deletedAt exists. Restore clears deletedAt. Background purge job runs daily for entries past tier retention.

after_state.description: Tier-aware soft-delete with restore + purge job.

files.purpose: Soft-delete via deletedAt and auto-filter from queries
Tier-aware delete + restore + trash list
Daily job hard-deletes entries past retention

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add deletedAt and pre hooks
Step 3: Ship trash UI for paid users
testing_strategy: Free tier hard delete: doc gone immediately. Paid soft: delete sets deletedAt, restore clears, queries hide while soft-deleted. Purge: backdate deletedAt 31 days, run job, assert hard-deleted.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would add isDeleted boolean - loses retention timing info. timestamp + auto-filter is cleaner.

title: Replace mongoose populate cascade with single aggregation across 3 levels
business_context: Org → Projects → Tasks → Assignees lookup uses .populate at 3 levels. p99 hits 1.4s. Need single aggregation.
constraints: Must preserve sort order across levels
reasoning: Single aggregation with $lookup at each level. Project filtering and pagination in subpipelines. Use projection to avoid loading large fields. Result shape preserved.

after_state.description: Single aggregation pipeline with projected subpipelines and sort preservation.

files.purpose: Single aggregation with limits at each level

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add new aggregation alongside populate
testing_strategy: Latency test: p99 drops dramatically. Result shape: deep-equal against populate response. Limits: 200 tasks per project cap respected.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would just add lean() everywhere - faster but same query count. Aggregation collapses them.

title: Prevent XML XXE and JSON prototype-pollution attacks across all body parsers
business_context: Security review found prototype pollution risks via __proto__ in JSON, and XXE via the optional XML body parser used by webhook receiver. Need parser hardening.
constraints: XML still required for one legacy partner

before_state.description: Default JSON parser + xml2js with default settings.

issues: XML XXE possible if external entities enabled

reasoning: Custom JSON reviver rejects __proto__/constructor/prototype keys. XML parser disables entity expansion (DTDs). Add size limit. Add Content-Length check.

after_state.description: Safe JSON reviver + XML with disabled entities + payload size guard.

files.purpose: JSON parser with reviver rejecting prototype-poisoning keys
XML parser with entities disabled and DTD rejection
Wires safe parsers in app composition

migration_strategy: Step 2: Add safe XML alongside legacy
Step 3: Communicate to partner that DTD payloads will be rejected
testing_strategy: Prototype test: POST { __proto__: { admin: true } } returns 400. XXE test: payload with external entity returns 400. Size test: payload > 1MB returns 413.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would delete __proto__ post-parse - the prototype pollution may already have happened during JSON.parse object construction.

title: Implement reconnecting Socket.IO client wrapper with exponential backoff and queued sends
business_context: On flaky networks Socket.IO disconnects; emits during disconnect are silently dropped. Need a wrapper that queues during disconnect and replays on reconnect with bounded queue.
constraints: Must surface connection state to UI
Must drop ephemeral events (like typing) but keep important ones

files.content:
import { io } from 'socket.io-client';
export const socket = io('/', { transports: ['websocket'] });

reasoning: Wrapper class with state machine (connecting/connected/disconnected). emit() takes flags { ephemeral, queueable }. Queued emits drain on reconnect. Backoff 1s,2s,4s,8s,16s,30s capped. Bounded queue (100 items) - drop oldest non-critical.

after_state.description: Reconnecting wrapper with bounded queue, flags, and state observable.

files.purpose: Reconnecting socket with bounded queue and state observable
UI banner reflecting connection state

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add wrapper alongside existing socket
Step 3: Add banner to app shell
testing_strategy: Disconnect-queue test: disconnect, emit queueable, reconnect, assert sent. Ephemeral test: emit ephemeral during disconnect, assert dropped. Overflow test: queue 200 items, assert capped.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would unbounded-buffer everything - app exhausts memory on long disconnects. Bounded queue with eviction is the right knob.

title: Replace toast-based form errors with structured field-error provider and inline display
business_context: Form errors shown via toasts that disappear before users can fix them. Need structured per-field error state that lives next to the relevant field.
constraints: Must work with existing useForm hook
Show summary at top of long forms
reasoning: FormErrorProvider stores error map per submission. Backend returns { error: { details: [{ path, message }] } }. Hook splits into field-level and form-level errors. Components access via useFieldError(name). Summary at top lists all errors.

after_state.description: FormErrorProvider with field-level access + summary.

files.purpose: Context for field errors with granular hooks
Inline display per field
Wraps form with provider and renders inline errors

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate forms one by one
Step 3: Remove toast.error for form submits
testing_strategy: Error map test: submit invalid, assert FieldError renders next to bad field. Clear test: successful submit clears errors. Summary test: 5 errors, assert summary lists all.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would push all errors into a single string at top of form - fine for short forms, painful for long ones.

title: Move runtime feature flags out of code into hot-reloadable config store
business_context: Flag rules baked in code mean restarting deploy to flip a flag. Want hot-reloadable flag store readable from Redis with a 5s cache. Operators flip flags without deploys.

files.content: export const flags = { NEW_CHECKOUT: true, AI_SUGGESTIONS: false };

reasoning: Flag rules stored in Redis hash. In-process cache with 5s TTL. Cache refresh via Redis pub/sub when admin writes. Admin endpoint writes rule, publishes invalidation.

after_state.description: Redis-backed flag store with pub/sub invalidation and in-memory cache.

files.purpose: Redis-backed flags with pub/sub invalidation
Admin endpoint with audit

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add Redis backing alongside code flags
testing_strategy: Hot reload test: write flag in instance A, instance B sees new value within 5s. Pub/sub test: simulate publish, assert cache cleared.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would fetch Redis on every request - 1ms+ overhead per call. In-process cache with invalidation gives near-zero latency.

title: Add 3D site-survey tool: drop pins on terrain with elevation queries
business_context: Field surveyors mark survey points on a 3D terrain mesh. Need point placement via raycaster, elevation lookup, persistent storage.
constraints: Pins persisted to backend on placement
Pins visible to other surveyors in real time
reasoning: Terrain GLB loaded. Raycast on click returns intersection with terrain mesh - x,y,z gives elevation. Pin rendered as small mesh at that point. Hover triggers elevation lookup via THREE.Raycaster vs invisible plane.

after_state.description: 3D terrain with raycast-placed pins and hover elevation.

files.purpose: Terrain with click-to-place pins, hover elevation, socket sync

migration_strategy: Step 1: Backend pin model + endpoints + socket
Step 2: Ship 3D viewer behind feature flag
Step 3: Add note editing per pin
testing_strategy: Click test: click terrain, assert pin appears at that position and POST fires. Live sync: client A places pin, client B sees it. Hover: assert elevation displayed near cursor.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would compute elevation from x,z by sampling a heightmap separately - drifts from mesh. Raycaster intersection is authoritative.

title: Add account-recovery flow with rate-limited email tokens and security checks
business_context: Forgot-password sends token via email but with no rate limit and no security questions. Need rate limit per email, token uniqueness, audit, and after-reset session invalidation.
reasoning: Always return 200 regardless of email existence. Hash token, store with TTL. Reset endpoint clears all refresh tokens (sessions). Rate limit per email via Redis. Audit each step.

after_state.description: Constant-response forgot flow, hashed expiring token, session invalidation, rate limit, audit.

files.purpose: Constant 200, rate-limited, hashed token, session invalidation, audit

testing_strategy: Enumeration test: forgot with valid and invalid email - both return 200 with similar timing. Rate limit: 4 requests in an hour returns 200 but no email after 3rd. Replay: use token, try again, assert INVALID. Session test: reset, assert all refresh tokens revoked.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would return 404 for unknown email - leaks existence. Or store plaintext token - DB leak compromises pending resets.

title: Add per-tenant webhook subscriptions with per-tenant secret and delivery isolation
business_context: All webhook subscriptions share single delivery pool. One tenant's failing endpoint slows others. Need per-tenant delivery isolation and per-tenant secrets.
constraints: No single failing tenant should impact others

files.content: export async function fire(payload: unknown) { /* enqueue to single queue */ }

reasoning: Per-tenant BullMQ queues - dynamically named webhook:${orgId}. Workers iterate active queues with bounded concurrency. Failed tenant retries don't block others. Secrets stored per-subscription.

after_state.description: Dynamic per-tenant queues with isolated worker concurrency.

files.purpose: Dynamic per-tenant queues; per-tenant worker concurrency limit
Fires into per-tenant queue

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate fire() to use per-tenant
Step 3: Spawn worker per active tenant on boot, refresh periodically
testing_strategy: Isolation: simulate tenant A endpoint failing, assert tenant B deliveries unaffected. Backoff: assert retries don't pile up across tenants.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would add concurrency=1 globally - solves blocking but starves all tenants. Per-tenant queues with per-tenant concurrency is correct.

title: Add response compression and HTTP/2 with proper SSE handling
business_context: Backend doesn't compress responses. JSON payloads are 5-10x larger than they need to be. Need brotli/gzip with carve-out for SSE streams and small bodies.
constraints: Compression level chosen for CPU vs size tradeoff
HTTP/2 must work with reverse proxy
reasoning: compression middleware with filter excluding event-stream content-type and small bodies. Use brotli when client supports it. HTTP/2 handled by reverse proxy (nginx).

after_state.description: Compression with SSE carve-out and threshold.

files.purpose: Compression with SSE skip and 1KB threshold
App-wide compression

migration_strategy: Step 2: Test SSE endpoints still stream
Step 3: Monitor CPU and payload sizes
testing_strategy: Compression test: large JSON returns with content-encoding header. SSE test: stream endpoint omits content-encoding. Threshold test: 500B response uncompressed.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would slap compression on all responses - SSE buffering by gzip breaks streaming.

title: Add observability instrumentation with OpenTelemetry traces, metrics, and structured logs
business_context: When something breaks, we have no idea why. Need traces (per request), metrics (Prometheus), and structured logs correlated by traceId. Cannot adopt a paid APM yet.
constraints: Must not regress p99 by more than 5ms
Trace must propagate to outbound HTTP
Cannot leak secret data into spans
reasoning: OpenTelemetry SDK with auto-instrumentation for Express, Mongoose, fetch. Spans get traceId/spanId. Pino logger with mixin injecting traceId. Prometheus metrics endpoint. Redact secret fields via attribute filter.

after_state.description: OTel auto-instrumentation, pino with trace correlation, Prometheus endpoint, attribute redaction.

files.purpose: OTel SDK with auto-instrumentation + secret redaction in headers
Pino logger injecting traceId and redacting secrets
Prometheus histogram + counters + endpoint
Wires tracing/logger/metrics in correct order

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate logger calls to pino
Step 5: Replace console.log with logger.* via lint rule
testing_strategy: Trace propagation: outbound fetch sees traceparent header. Log correlation: log message includes traceId matching the span. Metric test: hit endpoint, scrape /metrics, assert histogram observation.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would skip redaction and leak passwords into spans visible in tracing UI. Or initialize tracing after Express - misses auto-instrumentation hooks.

title: Add multi-room subscribe with shared single socket
business_context: App opens 5+ Socket.IO connections per tab (different features open different namespaces). Browsers limit to ~6 WS, mobile less. Need one socket multiplexed across features.

before_state.description: Each feature opens its own socket.

files.content:
import { io } from 'socket.io-client';
export function useChat() { const socket = io('/chat'); /* ... */ }

reasoning: Single shared socket on default namespace. Multiplex via channel prefixes in event names. SharedSocket wrapper exposes channel(name) returning a sub-API. Channel listeners auto-unsubscribe.

after_state.description: Single socket + multiplexed channels.

files.purpose: Shared socket with namespaced channels and per-channel disposal
Feature consumer using shared socket via channel

migration_strategy: Step 2: Migrate features one at a time
Step 3: Confirm only one WS in DevTools
testing_strategy: Connection count: open 3 features, assert only one WebSocket in network tab. Cleanup test: unmount, assert listeners removed.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would keep multiple namespaces but reuse socket-io's manager - reduces handshakes but still multiple connections. Channel multiplexing on one namespace beats it.

title: Split monorepo into apps/packages with strict boundary enforcement
business_context: Single src/ directory mixes shared utilities, server, client, scripts. Hard to enforce that client doesn't import from server. Need workspace layout with strict imports.
constraints: Imports across boundaries must be explicit
reasoning: pnpm workspaces with apps/server, apps/web, packages/shared. ESLint rule enforces that web doesn't import from server. Shared types live in packages/shared. tsconfig project references.

after_state.description: Workspace structure with project references and lint-enforced boundaries.

files.purpose: Workspace root manifest
Server app package
Web app package - explicitly does not depend on @app/server
Shared types/schemas package
ESLint enforces import boundary
TypeScript project references - incremental, dependency-aware builds

testing_strategy: Build test: pnpm build succeeds. Boundary test: import server from web, lint fails. Type test: shared types resolve in both apps.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would copy code into multiple folders without project references - tsc rebuilds everything every time. Project references give correct incremental builds.

title: Move global modals to declarative imperative bridge with stack management
business_context: Modals opened from anywhere via global state. Stack management is ad-hoc. Need a clean API: openModal(Component, props) returning a promise that resolves with the modal's result.

before_state.description: Each feature manages its own modal open state.

reasoning: Module-scoped modal stack + Provider that renders stack. openModal pushes onto stack and returns promise resolved by component via close(result).

after_state.description: Imperative openModal API + stack provider + per-modal promise.

files.purpose: Imperative openModal returning promise + stack-aware root
Modal receives close(result) from manager
Caller awaits modal result; no local modal state needed

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add ModalRoot at app root
Step 2: Migrate one dialog at a time
testing_strategy: Stack test: open A then B, escape closes B then A. Promise test: open + close(true), assert promise resolves true. Backdrop test: click backdrop closes top.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use a Context that re-renders the entire app on every modal change. Module-scoped store with useSyncExternalStore keeps re-renders local.

title: Add real-time vehicle tracking on 3D map with smooth movement interpolation
business_context: Fleet ops watches delivery vehicles on a map. GPS updates arrive at 5s intervals. Vehicles teleport on map between updates. Need smooth interpolation + 3D map with tilt.
reasoning: r3f canvas overlaying a base raster map. Vehicle as a small mesh. Each vehicle keeps current pos + target pos. In requestAnimationFrame, lerp toward target. Socket updates push new targets. Reconnect requests recent updates.

after_state.description: 3D vehicle layer with target-position lerp at 60fps.

files.purpose: Instanced vehicles with target-position lerp; reconnect requests recent updates

migration_strategy: Step 3: Add reconnect catchup via sinceMs
Step 4: Tune lerp factor for natural motion
testing_strategy: Smoothness: between updates, assert position smoothly approaches target. Capacity: 500 vehicles, assert 60fps. Reconnect: drop socket, reconnect, assert missed updates flow in via since.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would render one mesh per vehicle and update via React state per update - 500 setState per second is jank.

title: Add per-tenant secret scoping with envelope-encrypted org keys
business_context: Customer secrets (Stripe keys, API keys for their integrations) stored encrypted with a single app-wide KEK. Compromise of one tenant's secret reads everyone's. Need per-tenant DEKs from per-tenant KEKs.
constraints: Existing global-KEK secrets must keep working during migration
Rotating one tenant's KEK should not affect others
reasoning: Per-tenant KEK aliased in KMS. Per-secret DEK wrapped by tenant KEK. Lookup: load secret -> get encDek + tenantId -> decrypt DEK via tenant KEK -> decrypt payload. Old global-KEK secrets identified by 'kekVersion: global' continue to work during transition.

after_state.description: Tenant-scoped KEKs in KMS, per-secret DEKs.

files.purpose: Per-tenant envelope crypto
Service uses per-tenant encryption keyed by alias
Endpoints store and check secrets via tenant-scoped crypto

migration_strategy: Step 1: Provision per-tenant KEK aliases in KMS
Step 2: Re-encrypt existing secrets: load via global KEK, re-encrypt with tenant KEK
testing_strategy: Per-tenant isolation: tenant A's secrets cannot be decrypted with tenant B's KEK. Rotation: rotate tenant A's KEK, assert tenant B unaffected. Tamper test: modify ct, assert GCM auth tag fails.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would keep one KEK and namespace by prefix - compromise of KEK exposes all. Per-tenant KEK with KMS-managed aliases is the proper boundary.
